verses.latin_vulgate: Petrus autem et Joannes ascendebant in templum ad horam orationis nonam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτό δὲ πέτρος καὶ ἰωάννης ἀνέβαινον εἰς τὸ ἱερὸν ἐπὶ τὴν ὥραν τῆς προσευχῆς τὴν ἐννάτην
verses.king_james: Now Peter and John went up together into the temple at the hour of prayer, *being* the ninth *hour*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quidam vir, qui erat claudus ex utero matris suæ, bajulabatur: quem ponebant quotidie ad portam templi, quæ dicitur Speciosa, ut peteret eleemosynam ab introëuntibus in templum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καί τις ἀνὴρ χωλὸς ἐκ κοιλίας μητρὸς αὐτοῦ ὑπάρχων ἐβαστάζετο ὃν ἐτίθουν καθ᾽ ἡμέραν πρὸς τὴν θύραν τοῦ ἱεροῦ τὴν λεγομένην ὡραίαν τοῦ αἰτεῖν ἐλεημοσύνην παρὰ τῶν εἰσπορευομένων εἰς τὸ ἱερόν
verses.king_james: And a certain man lame from his mother's womb was carried, whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask alms of them that entered into the temple;

verses.latin_vulgate: Is cum vidisset Petrum et Joannem incipientes introire in templum, rogabat ut eleemosynam acciperet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς ἰδὼν πέτρον καὶ ἰωάννην μέλλοντας εἰσιέναι εἰς τὸ ἱερὸν ἠρώτα ἐλεημοσύνην λαβεῖν
verses.king_james: Who seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple asked an alms.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intuens autem in eum Petrus cum Joanne, dixit: Respice in nos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀτενίσας δὲ πέτρος εἰς αὐτὸν σὺν τῷ ἰωάννῃ εἶπεν βλέψον εἰς ἡμᾶς
verses.king_james: And Peter, fastening his eyes upon him with John, said, Look on us.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille intendebat in eos, sperans se aliquid accepturum ab eis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἐπεῖχεν αὐτοῖς προσδοκῶν τι παρ᾽ αὐτῶν λαβεῖν
verses.king_james: And he gave heed unto them, expecting to receive something of them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Petrus autem dixit: Argentum et aurum non est mihi: quod autem habeo, hoc tibi do: in nomine Jesu Christi Nazareni surge, et ambula.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ πέτρος ἀργύριον καὶ χρυσίον οὐχ ὑπάρχει μοι ὃ δὲ ἔχω τοῦτό σοι δίδωμι ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τοῦ ναζωραίου ἐγεῖραι καὶ περιπάτει
verses.king_james: Then Peter said, Silver and gold have I none; but such as I have give I thee: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et apprehensa manu ejus dextera, allevavit eum, et protinus consolidatæ sunt bases ejus et plantæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πιάσας αὐτὸν τῆς δεξιᾶς χειρὸς ἤγειρεν παραχρῆμα δὲ ἐστερεώθησαν αὐτοῦ αἱ βάσεις καὶ τὰ σφῦρα
verses.king_james: And he took him by the right hand, and lifted *him* up: and immediately his feet and ankle bones received strength.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exiliens stetit, et ambulabat: et intravit cum illis in templum ambulans, et exiliens, et laudans Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξαλλόμενος ἔστη καὶ περιεπάτει καὶ εἰσῆλθεν σὺν αὐτοῖς εἰς τὸ ἱερὸν περιπατῶν καὶ ἁλλόμενος καὶ αἰνῶν τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: And he leaping up stood, and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking, and leaping, and praising God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vidit omnis populus eum ambulantem et laudantem Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶδεν αὐτὸν πᾶς ὁ λαὸς περιπατοῦντα καὶ αἰνοῦντα τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: And all the people saw him walking and praising God:

verses.latin_vulgate: Cognoscebant autem illum, quod ipse erat qui ad eleemosynam sedebat ad Speciosam portam templi: et impleti sunt stupore et extasi in eo quod contigerat illi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπεγίνωσκον τε αὐτὸν ὅτι οὑτὸς ἦν ὁ πρὸς τὴν ἐλεημοσύνην καθήμενος ἐπὶ τῇ ὡραίᾳ πύλῃ τοῦ ἱεροῦ καὶ ἐπλήσθησαν θάμβους καὶ ἐκστάσεως ἐπὶ τῷ συμβεβηκότι αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And they knew that it was he which sat for alms at the Beautiful gate of the temple: and they were filled with wonder and amazement at that which had happened unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum teneret autem Petrum et Joannem, cucurrit omnis populus ad eos ad porticum quæ appellatur Salomonis, stupentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κρατοῦντος δὲ τοῦ ἰαθἐντος χωλοῦ τὸν πέτρον καὶ ἰωάννην συνέδραμεν πρὸς αὐτοὺς πᾶς ὁ λαὸς ἐπὶ τῇ στοᾷ τῇ καλουμένῃ σολομῶντος ἔκθαμβοι
verses.king_james: And as the lame man which was healed held Peter and John, all the people ran together unto them in the porch that is called Solomon's, greatly wondering.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns autem Petrus, respondit ad populum: Viri Israëlitæ, quid miramini in hoc, aut nos quid intuemini, quasi nostra virtute aut potestate fecerimus hunc ambulare?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδὼν δὲ πέτρος ἀπεκρίνατο πρὸς τὸν λαόν ἄνδρες ἰσραηλῖται τί θαυμάζετε ἐπὶ τούτῳ ἢ ἡμῖν τί ἀτενίζετε ὡς ἰδίᾳ δυνάμει ἢ εὐσεβείᾳ πεποιηκόσιν τοῦ περιπατεῖν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And when Peter saw *it*, he answered unto the people, Ye men of Israel, why marvel ye at this? or why look ye so earnestly on us, as though by our own power or holiness we had made this man to walk?

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus Abraham, et Deus Isaac, et Deus Jacob, Deus patrum nostrorum glorificavit filium suum Jesum, quem vos quidem tradidistis, et negastis ante faciem Pilati, judicante illo dimitti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ θεὸς ἀβραὰμ καὶ ἰσαὰκ καὶ ἰακώβ ὁ θεὸς τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν ἐδόξασεν τὸν παῖδα αὐτοῦ ἰησοῦν ὃν ὑμεῖς παρεδώκατε καὶ ἠρνήσασθε αὐτόν κατὰ πρόσωπον πιλάτου κρίναντος ἐκείνου ἀπολύειν
verses.king_james: The God of Abraham, and of Isaac, and of Jacob, the God of our fathers, hath glorified his Son Jesus; whom ye delivered up, and denied him in the presence of Pilate, when he was determined to let *him* go.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos autem sanctum et justum negastis, et petistis virum homicidam donari vobis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ τὸν ἅγιον καὶ δίκαιον ἠρνήσασθε καὶ ᾐτήσασθε ἄνδρα φονέα χαρισθῆναι ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: But ye denied the Holy One and the Just, and desired a murderer to be granted unto you;

verses.latin_vulgate: auctorem vero vitæ interfecistis, quem Deus suscitavit a mortuis, cujus nos testes sumus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν δὲ ἀρχηγὸν τῆς ζωῆς ἀπεκτείνατε ὃν ὁ θεὸς ἤγειρεν ἐκ νεκρῶν οὗ ἡμεῖς μάρτυρές ἐσμεν
verses.king_james: And killed the Prince of life, whom God hath raised from the dead; whereof we are witnesses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et in fide nominis ejus, hunc quem vos vidistis et nostis, confirmavit nomen ejus: et fides, quæ per eum est, dedit integram sanitatem istam in conspectu omnium vestrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπὶ τῇ πίστει τοῦ ὀνόματος αὐτοῦ τοῦτον ὃν θεωρεῖτε καὶ οἴδατε ἐστερέωσεν τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἡ πίστις ἡ δι᾽ αὐτοῦ ἔδωκεν αὐτῷ τὴν ὁλοκληρίαν ταύτην ἀπέναντι πάντων ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And his name through faith in his name hath made this man strong, whom ye see and know: yea, the faith which is by him hath given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc, fratres, scio quia per ignorantiam fecistis, sicut et principes vestri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ νῦν ἀδελφοί οἶδα ὅτι κατὰ ἄγνοιαν ἐπράξατε ὥσπερ καὶ οἱ ἄρχοντες ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And now, brethren, I wot that through ignorance ye did *it*, as *did* also your rulers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem, quæ prænuntiavit per os omnium prophetarum, pati Christum suum, sic implevit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ θεὸς ἃ προκατήγγειλεν διὰ στόματος πάντων τῶν προφητῶν αὐτοῦ παθεῖν τὸν χριστὸν ἐπλήρωσεν οὕτως
verses.king_james: But those things, which God before had shewed by the mouth of all his prophets, that Christ should suffer, he hath so fulfilled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pœnitemini igitur et convertimini, ut deleantur peccata vestra: ut cum venerint tempora refrigerii a conspectu Domini,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετανοήσατε οὖν καὶ ἐπιστρέψατε εἰς τὸ ἐξαλειφθῆναι ὑμῶν τὰς ἁμαρτίας ὅπως ἄν ἔλθωσιν καιροί ἀναψύξεως ἀπό προσώπου τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out, when the times of refreshing shall come from the presence of the Lord;

verses.latin_vulgate: et miserit eum qui prædicatus est vobis, Jesum Christum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποστείλῃ τὸν προκεκηρυγμένον ὑμῖν ἰησοῦν χριστόν
verses.king_james: And he shall send Jesus Christ, which before was preached unto you:

verses.latin_vulgate: quem oportet quidem cælum suscipere usque in tempora restitutionis omnium quæ locutus est Deus per os sanctorum suorum a sæculo prophetarum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν δεῖ οὐρανὸν μὲν δέξασθαι ἄχρι χρόνων ἀποκαταστάσεως πάντων ὧν ἐλάλησεν ὁ θεὸς διὰ στόματος πάντων, ἁγίων αὐτοῦ προφητῶν ἀπ᾽ αἰῶνος
verses.king_james: Whom the heaven must receive until the times of restitution of all things, which God hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began.

verses.latin_vulgate: Moyses quidem dixit: Quoniam prophetam suscitabit vobis Dominus Deus vester de fratribus vestris, tamquam me: ipsum audietis juxta omnia quæcumque locutus fuerit vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μωσῆς μὲν γὰρ πρὸς τούς πατέρας εἶπεν ὅτι προφήτην ὑμῖν ἀναστήσει κύριος ὁ θεὸς ὑμῶν ἐκ τῶν ἀδελφῶν ὑμῶν ὡς ἐμέ αὐτοῦ ἀκούσεσθε κατὰ πάντα ὅσα ἂν λαλήσῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: For Moses truly said unto the fathers, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erit autem: omnis anima quæ non audierit prophetam illum, exterminabitur de plebe.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔσται δὲ πᾶσα ψυχὴ ἥτις ἂν μὴ ἀκούσῃ τοῦ προφήτου ἐκείνου ἐξολοθρευθήσεται ἐκ τοῦ λαοῦ
verses.king_james: And it shall come to pass, *that* every soul, which will not hear that prophet, shall be destroyed from among the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnes prophetæ, a Samuel et deinceps, qui locuti sunt, annuntiaverunt dies istos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάντες δὲ οἱ προφῆται ἀπὸ σαμουὴλ καὶ τῶν καθεξῆς ὅσοι ἐλάλησαν καὶ προκατήγγειλαν τὰς ἡμέρας ταύτας
verses.king_james: Yea, and all the prophets from Samuel and those that follow after, as many as have spoken, have likewise foretold of these days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos estis filii prophetarum, et testamenti quod disposuit Deus ad patres nostros, dicens ad Abraham: Et in semine tuo benedicentur omnes familiæ terræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς ἐστε υἱοὶ τῶν προφητῶν καὶ τῆς διαθήκης ἡς διέθετο ὁ θεὸς πρὸς τοὺς πατέρας ἡμῶν λέγων πρὸς ἀβραάμ, καὶ τῷ σπέρματί σου ἐνευλογηθήσονται πᾶσαι αἱ πατριαὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: Ye are the children of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying unto Abraham, And in thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vobis primum Deus suscitans filium suum, misit eum benedicentem vobis: ut convertat se unusquisque a nequitia sua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμῖν πρῶτον ὁ θεὸς ἀναστήσας τὸν παῖδα αὐτοῦ ἰησοῦν, ἀπέστειλεν αὐτὸν εὐλογοῦντα ὑμᾶς ἐν τῷ ἀποστρέφειν ἕκαστον ἀπὸ τῶν πονηριῶν ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Unto you first God, having raised up his Son Jesus, sent him to bless you, in turning away every one of you from his iniquities.
